# AWS EKS HyperPod Receiver

The AWS EKS HyperPod receiver collects node health status metrics from
[Amazon SageMaker HyperPod](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/sagemaker-hyperpod.html)
clusters running on Amazon EKS. It watches Kubernetes node labels set by the
HyperPod health monitoring agent and emits gauge metrics representing each
node's current health state.

## How it works

SageMaker HyperPod attaches a `sagemaker.amazonaws.com/node-health-status` label
to each managed node. The receiver uses the Kubernetes API (via an in-cluster client)
to watch node labels and converts the health status into four one-hot encoded gauge
metrics per node. Exactly one metric has value `1` (the current state); the other
three have value `0`.

This one-hot encoding allows straightforward PromQL aggregation (e.g.,
`sum(hyperpod_node_health_status_unschedulable)` counts all unhealthy nodes).

## Configuration

```yaml
receivers:
  awsekshyperpodreceiver:
    # How often to scrape node health status. Default: 60s.
    collection_interval: 60s
    # EKS cluster name. Added as a "cluster_name" attribute on all metrics.
    # If empty, the attribute is omitted.
    cluster_name: "my-hyperpod-cluster"
```

| Parameter | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `collection_interval` | How often to poll Kubernetes node labels | `60s` |
| `cluster_name` | EKS cluster name added as a datapoint attribute | `""` (omitted) |

## Metrics

All metrics are gauges with value `0` or `1`. For each node, exactly one metric
has value `1` (the node's current health state) and the remaining three have value `0`.

| Metric Name | Description | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| `hyperpod_node_health_status_schedulable` | Node is healthy and schedulable | 1 |
| `hyperpod_node_health_status_unschedulable_pending_replacement` | Node is unschedulable, pending hardware replacement | 1 |
| `hyperpod_node_health_status_unschedulable_pending_reboot` | Node is unschedulable, pending reboot | 1 |
| `hyperpod_node_health_status_unschedulable` | Node is unschedulable | 1 |

## Datapoint Attributes

| Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| `node_name` | Kubernetes node name (e.g., `hyperpod-i-0123456789abcdef0`) |
| `instance_id` | EC2 instance ID with `hyperpod-` prefix stripped (e.g., `i-0123456789abcdef0`) |
| `cluster_name` | EKS cluster name from config (omitted if config value is empty) |

## Prerequisites

- The collector must run inside the EKS cluster (in-cluster Kubernetes client).
- The service account must have `get`, `list`, and `watch` permissions on `nodes`.
- Nodes must have the `sagemaker.amazonaws.com/node-health-status` label set by the
  HyperPod health monitoring agent. Nodes without this label are silently skipped.

## Example RBAC

```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: hyperpod-health-reader
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["nodes"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
```
